trm:term.trm:name: Diagnostic Issue
trm:term.trm:definition: A structured failure-mode record with condition, cause, remedy, severity, and optional verification links. Diagnostic issues capture troubleshooting knowledge as queryable domain data instead of prose-only documentation.

trm:term.trm:name: Deliberation Choice Set
trm:term.trm:definition: A structured set of options under consideration, with per-option outcomes, conditions, and rationale. Choice sets model alternatives before, during, or outside formal architecture decisions.

pr:section.pr:title: Diagnostic Layer
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Known conditions, causes, remedies, severities, and verification links for failure modes.
pr:section.pr:p: Troubleshooting imported from DITA looks like prose, but its useful information is operational: a condition is observed, one or more causes explain it, and remedies describe recovery. That structure should be queryable across the spec. The diagnostic layer owns this domain, while prose can still embed or render diagnostic issues inside relevant documentation sections.

A diag:issue may point at the node it diagnoses via @ref. Conditions, causes, and remedies accept rich mixed content so authors can include prose phrases, terminology references, source citations, content references, or command snippets.

diag:issue.diag:title: Reference validation fails
diag:issue.diag:condition: The validator reports that a key reference does not resolve.
diag:issue.diag:cause: A layer contains a reference attribute whose value does not match any node ID in the combined document.
diag:issue.diag:remedy: Check the spelling of the reference value and confirm the target file is listed in the index manifest.

pr:section.pr:title: Deliberation Layer
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Choice sets, options, outcomes, conditions, and rationale for tradeoffs that may or may not become decisions.
pr:section.pr:p: DITA task choices and DocBook-style option lists are not purely presentational. They often encode a domain model: several options are available under different conditions, some are selected, some are rejected, and some remain deferred. Architecture decisions are one resolved form of this broader deliberation domain, but not every choice is an ADR.

The deliberation layer captures option sets without forcing them into the decision lifecycle. A delib:choice-set can remain open, be resolved by selecting one or more options, or be referenced by a later dec:decision. Prose should use choice sets for meaningful tradeoffs and keep ordinary ordered lists for simple authoring order.

delib:choice-set.delib:title: Split DITA-inspired elements by semantic domain

delib:option.delib:title: Keep all DITA-inspired elements in prose
delib:option.delib:rationale: This preserves authoring convenience but hides troubleshooting and option semantics from graph and query tooling.

delib:option.delib:title: Keep authoring shape in prose and move domain data to content, diagnostic, and deliberation layers
delib:option.delib:rationale: Figures remain prose, media identity lives in content, failure modes become diagnostics, and meaningful option sets become deliberations.

pr:section.pr:title: Content-Backed Media
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Figures stay in prose, while reusable media identity, location, media type, and hash live in the content layer.
pr:section.pr:p: Media imported from DocBook belongs to two domains. The figure, caption, and placement are prose authoring structures. The asset itself is reusable content: it has a URL or embedded body, a media type, and a hash. Therefore pr:media should point to cnt:content when the asset is reusable or integrity checked.

org:concept.org:purpose: Explains why troubleshooting belongs in a diagnostic domain with queryable conditions, causes, remedies, and severity.
Explains why meaningful choices and tradeoffs belong in a deliberation domain adjacent to, but broader than, decisions.
Explains how prose figures reuse content-addressed media nodes from the content layer.

rel:relation.rel:note: The diagnostic layer extracts troubleshooting semantics from technical prose while preserving rich rendering.
The layer is centered on the diagnostic issue vocabulary.
The sample diagnostic issue describes a validation failure mode.
The sample issue demonstrates diagnostic-layer structure.
Deliberation generalizes option sets and tradeoffs; architecture decisions are resolved deliberations with lifecycle status.
The layer is centered on the choice-set vocabulary.
The sample choice set records the domain split for technical documentation prose.
The sample choice set demonstrates deliberation-layer structure.
The rejected option belongs to the domain split choice set.
The selected option belongs to the domain split choice set.
Reusable media assets are content-addressed by the content layer.
The sample media content node demonstrates content-backed figure assets.

spec:clayers.llm:node: The diagnostic layer owns troubleshooting and remediation knowledge: conditions, causes, remedies, severity, responsible party, and optional verification links. Use diag:issue for known failure modes. Prose may embed diagnostic issues for rendering, but diagnostic structure is not prose-only authoring markup.

The deliberation layer owns meaningful choices and tradeoffs. Use delib:choice-set when options have conditions, outcomes, rationale, or may later be promoted into a decision. Keep dec:decision for ADR-style lifecycle metadata and accepted/rejected architectural decisions. Each option is a subordinate node that refines its containing choice set.

Content-backed media means pr:figure owns presentation context while pr:media references cnt:content for the asset. The content layer owns URL/body, media-type, and hash so screenshots, diagrams, and other media can be reused with integrity guarantees.
spec:clayers.llm:schema: Diagnostic issues are first-class nodes for known failure modes. Each diag:issue has id, optional ref to the affected node, title, severity, condition, one or more causes, one or more remedies, optional responsible party, and optional verifies-with links to tests.

Deliberation choice sets are first-class nodes for options and tradeoffs. Each delib:choice-set has id, optional ref to the topic being discussed, title, status, optional context, and one or more delib:option children. Options have ids, outcomes, titles, optional conditions, and rationale.

art:mapping.art:note: Diagnostic schema for issue, condition, cause, remedy, severity, responsible party, and verification links.
Documentation renderer for diagnostic issue cards and condition/cause/remedy fields.
Deliberation schema for choice sets, options, statuses, outcomes, conditions, and rationale.
Documentation renderer for deliberation choice sets and option outcomes.
Prose media references content nodes for reusable assets while retaining figure/caption authoring structure.
Documentation renderer resolves pr:media content references to cnt:content URLs and media types.
Postprocess transform synthesizes reference relations from diagnostic issues, diagnostic verification links, deliberation choice sets, content refs, and content-backed media.
Main documentation stylesheet imports diagnostic and deliberation renderers and declares their namespaces.
Embedded asset list includes diagnostic and deliberation schemas plus their documentation stylesheets for adopt/doc generation.
Namespace registry includes diagnostic and deliberation layer URNs and prefixes so combined documents serialize and query correctly.
